title: Operation Epic Fury - US Launches Massive Strikes on Iran
description: US and Israeli forces launch Operation Epic Fury with over 200 cruise missiles and stealth bomber strikes targeting Iranian military and nuclear sites across Tehran, Isfahan, Qom, and Karaj. This marks the beginning of the 2026 Iran War.
impact: War officially begins. Global markets crash. Oil jumps 15% overnight.

sources.name: CENTCOM Official Statement
Reuters

title: Iran Retaliates - Missile Strikes Hit US Bases and Israel
description: Iran launches massive retaliatory strikes with hundreds of ballistic missiles targeting US military installations in Iraq, Kuwait, and Bahrain, as well as Israeli military and infrastructure targets.
impact: 6 US servicemembers killed at Camp Buehring. First US combat deaths. Escalation risk skyrockets.

sources.name: Pentagon Briefing
AP News

title: Strait of Hormuz Blockade - 20% of Global Oil Supply Cut
description: Iran deploys naval forces, mines, and anti-ship missiles to block the Strait of Hormuz. Over 150 vessels stopped. An estimated 15 million barrels per day of oil transit is disrupted — roughly 20% of global supply.
impact: Oil prices surge past $85/barrel. Shipping insurance for Persian Gulf canceled by major insurers. Global supply chain disruption begins.

sources.name: Lloyd's List Maritime Intelligence
Bloomberg Energy

title: Qatar LNG Infrastructure Attacked - 20% of Global LNG Offline
description: Iranian drone swarms strike Qatari LNG export terminals at Ras Laffan, taking approximately 20% of global LNG supply offline. European natural gas prices spike immediately.
impact: European natural gas prices surge 38%. Energy crisis fears grip Europe and Asia. Qatar halts all LNG shipments.

sources.name: Qatar Energy Ministry
Financial Times

title: Qatari F-15s Shoot Down Iranian Jets - Gulf States Enter Combat
description: Qatari F-15QAs intercept and shoot down Iranian Su-24 attack aircraft approaching Al-Udeid Air Base. This marks the first time a Gulf state has engaged in direct air-to-air combat with Iran.
impact: Gulf states drawn into direct combat role. Regional war expansion confirmed. Gulf Cooperation Council activates mutual defense clause.

sources.name: Qatar MOD Statement
The War Zone

title: IRINS Dena Frigate Sunk - Major Naval Escalation
description: US Navy sinks the Iranian frigate IRINS Dena in the Persian Gulf after it fired anti-ship missiles at a US carrier group. First major naval engagement since the Tanker War of the 1980s.
impact: Persian Gulf becomes active naval combat zone. US deploys additional carrier group. Maritime insurance rates triple.

sources.name: US Navy Press Release
Naval News

title: Hezbollah Enters the War - Rockets Hit Northern Israel
description: Hezbollah declares war on Israel and launches hundreds of rockets from southern Lebanon into northern Israel, opening a second front. Israeli Defense Forces activate northern border reserves.
impact: Multi-front war confirmed. Israeli forces stretched across 3 fronts. Northern Israeli cities evacuated. 85,000 Lebanese displaced.

sources.name: IDF Spokesperson
Al Jazeera

title: Iranian UAVs Strike Azerbaijan - New Theater Opens
description: Iranian armed drones strike targets in Nakhchivan, Azerbaijan, marking the first time the conflict has expanded beyond the immediate Middle East theater. Azerbaijan condemns the strikes and recalls its ambassador.
impact: Conflict threatens to spill into South Caucasus. Turkey (NATO member) put on alert as Azerbaijan ally. NATO Article 5 discussions begin.

sources.name: Azerbaijan MFA
Caucasus Watch

title: Global Market Crash - Dow Drops 1,200 Points
description: Global financial markets experience severe sell-off as the Iran war escalates. Dow Jones drops over 1,200 points. Oil surges to multi-year highs. Gold breaks records. Crypto markets tumble as risk-off sentiment dominates.
impact: Trillions in market value erased. Flight to safety assets. Federal Reserve issues emergency statement. Oil above $85/barrel.

sources.name: Wall Street Journal
CNBC

title: Iran Declares Readiness for 'Long-Term War'
description: Iranian Supreme National Security Council publicly declares Iran is prepared for a prolonged military conflict. Military spokesman announces activation of all reserve forces and underground missile facilities.
impact: Signals conflict will not end quickly. Peace/ceasefire prospects diminish. International pressure mounts for diplomatic off-ramp.

sources.name: Press TV
BBC Persian

title: Iraqi Militias Launch Missiles at US Bases in Iraq
description: Iran-backed Iraqi militia groups (PMF/Hashd al-Shaabi) launch coordinated missile and drone attacks on US positions at Al-Asad Air Base and Erbil. Multiple waves over 48 hours.
impact: Iraq becomes active combat zone again. US forced to defend on multiple fronts simultaneously. Iraqi government unable to stop militia actions.

sources.name: CENTCOM
Iraq Oil Report

title: Israel Targets Iranian Oil Infrastructure - Energy War Begins
description: Israel strikes Tehran oil storage facility near key refinery, marking a strategic shift to targeting Iran's energy infrastructure. Massive fires visible across the city. This escalation threatens to further destabilize global energy markets.
impact: Oil prices surge past $93/barrel. Israel targeting energy infrastructure signals extended campaign. Iran's refinery capacity degrading.

sources.name: CBS News
Al Jazeera

title: Trump Signals Ground Troops - Demands Unconditional Surrender
description: President Trump says ground troops 'possible' in Iran, demands 'unconditional surrender.' NBC reports Trump privately expressed serious interest in ground deployment. This represents the most significant escalation signal since the war began.
impact: Ground invasion possibility sends shockwaves through diplomatic channels. Iran vows to resist. European allies express alarm.

sources.name: Bloomberg
NBC News

title: IRGC Attacks Oil Tankers in Strait of Hormuz - 8+ Vessels Damaged
description: IRGC claims hits on oil tankers Prima and Louise P with drones. At least 8 commercial vessels damaged since blockade began. A tugboat dispatched to assist a stricken vessel was struck by two missiles, reportedly killing all 8 crew members.
impact: Active attacks on commercial shipping effectively enforce total blockade. Maritime insurance completely withdrawn. 280 bulkers trapped in region.

sources.name: Al Jazeera
Wikipedia

title: Iran Claims Capture of US Soldiers - Information War Intensifies
description: Ali Larijani, head of Iran's National Security Council, claims Iran captured US soldiers. Trump administration denies reports. Meanwhile, CIA reportedly arming Kurdish forces in western Iran to spark uprising. Information warfare reaching new intensity.
impact: POW claims, whether true or false, dramatically escalate political pressure. Kurdish arming operation could open new internal front.

sources.name: Al Jazeera
CNN

title: Oil Smashes Through $100/Barrel — Hormuz Effectively Closed
description: Brent crude hits $119.50 intraday as the Strait of Hormuz is effectively closed to commercial traffic. Over 310 vessels now at anchor across the Persian Gulf and Gulf of Oman. US gasoline at $3.48/gal (AAA) and rising — pump prices lag crude by 1-2 weeks, projected to hit $4+/gal.
impact: Global energy crisis deepens. $119.50/barrel is highest price since 2008. Fuel rationing discussions begin in Europe and Asia. Analysts warn of $4+/gal gas as crude surge hits pumps.

sources.name: Bloomberg Energy
Reuters

title: Mojtaba Khamenei Named New Supreme Leader
description: Ali Khamenei's son Mojtaba Khamenei is elected as Iran's new Supreme Leader by the Assembly of Experts. In his first address, he vows continuation of the war and pledges to avenge his father's death. The appointment signals regime consolidation rather than collapse.
impact: Hereditary succession consolidates hardliner control. Ceasefire prospects diminish further. Western hopes of regime fracture appear premature.

sources.name: BBC Persian
Al Jazeera

title: Hezbollah Drone Strikes RAF Akrotiri, Cyprus — NATO Territory Hit
description: Hezbollah launches drone strikes on RAF Akrotiri in Cyprus, marking the first attack on a NATO member's sovereign territory in the conflict. The runway is damaged but no casualties are reported. French President Macron declares that an attack on Cyprus is an attack on all of Europe.
impact: First strike on NATO sovereign territory. NATO Article 5 discussions intensify. European nations reassess involvement. Macron's statement raises prospect of European military response.

sources.name: France 24
BBC

title: Saudi Arabia First Civilian Deaths — Al-Kharj Strike
description: An Iranian projectile strikes the Al-Kharj area of Saudi Arabia, killing 2 Saudi civilians and injuring 12. These are the first Saudi civilian casualties in the conflict. Saudi Arabia had been previously attacked but sustained no fatalities until now.
impact: Saudi civilian deaths risk drawing Riyadh into direct combat operations. Saudi Crown Prince MBS convenes emergency defense council. Gulf state populations demand retaliation.

sources.name: Al Arabiya
Reuters

title: Switzerland Declares US-Israel Strikes Violate International Law
description: Swiss Defense Minister Pfister formally condemns the US-Israel military campaign, declaring the strikes violate international law. Switzerland becomes the first traditionally neutral Western nation to formally condemn the US-Israel campaign.
impact: First neutral Western nation to condemn US-Israel operations. International legal pressure mounts. Could influence ICC proceedings and broader European diplomatic stance.

sources.name: Swissinfo
Reuters

title: Senate Fails to Stop War — Trump Gets Unlimited Authorization
description: The US Senate voted down a war powers resolution that would have forced Trump to withdraw from Iran operations. The resolution's failure gives the administration indefinite authorization to continue military action without Congressional approval.
impact: No Congressional check on military operations. Trump can escalate indefinitely. Anti-war movement suffers major setback. Hegseth signals 4-8 week campaign.

sources.name: CNBC
AP News

title: NATO Intercepts Second Iranian Missile Over Turkey
description: NATO air defense systems intercepted a second Iranian ballistic missile over Turkish airspace near Gaziantep. Turkey deployed six F-16s and air defense systems to Northern Cyprus. The incident further risks drawing NATO into direct conflict with Iran.
impact: Second missile incident over NATO territory. Article 5 discussions intensifying. Turkey deploying additional military assets to region. Risk of NATO direct involvement growing.

sources.name: Al Jazeera
Reuters

title: AI-Generated Fake Satellite Imagery Goes Viral — Information War Escalates
description: AFP confirmed AI-generated fake satellite imagery showing a destroyed US base in Qatar was spread by an Iranian news outlet. The fabricated image was detected via Google's SynthID watermark but garnered millions of views in multiple languages before being debunked.
impact: New form of AI-powered wartime disinformation emerging. Undermines trust in legitimate OSINT. Over 100 hacktivist Telegram channels active. Iran's internet at 4% of normal capacity.

sources.name: AFP / Arab News
Channels Television

title: Turkey: NATO Air Defenses Shoot Down Iranian Missile Over Turkish Airspace
description: NATO Patriot batteries deployed in southeastern Turkey shot down an Iranian ballistic missile over Turkish airspace near Gaziantep, marking the first time NATO air defenses have engaged a live Iranian threat. Turkey deployed six F-16s and additional air defense systems to Northern Cyprus. The intercept raises the stakes for potential NATO Article 5 invocation.
impact: First NATO combat engagement against Iranian missiles. Article 5 discussions accelerating. Turkey deploying additional military assets. Risk of direct NATO-Iran confrontation at highest level since conflict began.

sources.name: Al Jazeera
Reuters

title: Trump Calls War 'Little Excursion' — Signals Rapid Conclusion
description: President Trump described the Iran war as 'a little excursion' that would be 'finished pretty quickly' during a press conference on March 9. Meanwhile, Defense Secretary Hegseth declared the Iranian Navy is 'largely no more' and signaled a 4-8 week campaign. The contradictory messaging between quick resolution and extended campaign creates uncertainty about US strategy.
impact: Mixed signals on war duration create strategic ambiguity. Markets uncertain about conflict timeline. Iran's Araghchi rejects ceasefire on NBC, saying 'We need to continue fighting for the sake of our people.'

sources.name: CBS News
NBC News

title: Hegseth Warns 'Most Intense Day of Strikes' — Iran Vows 'Eye for Eye' on Infrastructure
description: US Defense Secretary Hegseth warned that Day 12 will be the most intense day of strikes against Iran since the war began, as CENTCOM confirmed 3,000+ targets struck. Simultaneously, Iran vowed 'eye for an eye' retaliation if US-Israel continues hitting oil and energy infrastructure, threatening to target Israeli and Gulf state energy assets. 40 killed in eastern Tehran residential buildings earlier in the day.
impact: Major escalation on both sides. US committing to intensified aerial campaign while Iran threatens symmetric infrastructure strikes. 40 civilian deaths in Tehran residential buildings and Isfahan UNESCO heritage sites damaged. Risk of all-out infrastructure war growing. Ceasefire contacts from China, Russia, France rejected by Iran.

sources.name: PBS
Al Jazeera

title: Global Food Crisis Looms — Fertilizer Prices Spike 33% as Hormuz Blocks Supply
description: The UN warned that the Strait of Hormuz closure is not only disrupting oil markets but also threatening global food security. Approximately 33% of globally traded fertilizer transits the Strait. Urea prices spiked from $450 to $600/tonne in one week. Wheat hit its highest level in two years, palm oil at its highest in over a year. G7 finance ministers planned emergency meeting to discuss joint strategic oil reserve release.
impact: Food and fertilizer crisis emerging alongside energy crisis. Planting season disrupted globally. Wheat +56%, corn +37%, soybeans +43% from pre-war levels. Grocery bills up ~34%. Developing countries face acute food insecurity risk. IMF chief warns of global inflation.

sources.name: Bloomberg
Al Jazeera

title: Iran Uses Cluster Munitions Against Israeli Civilians — Banned Under International Law
description: IDF confirmed approximately half of the 300 missiles Iran fired at Israel carried cluster bomb warheads, banned under the 2008 Convention on Cluster Munitions. A cluster munition strike hit 6 sites across central Israel, killing 2 people and seriously wounding 1. Use of cluster munitions against civilian areas constitutes a potential war crime under international humanitarian law.
impact: Iran's use of banned cluster munitions marks a significant escalation in weaponry targeting civilian areas. 186 total attack waves against Israel since war began. International condemnation expected. Potential war crimes investigation.

sources.name: Times of Israel
NBC News

title: Oil Prices Crash 11-12% — Trump Signals War Could End 'Very Soon'
description: Brent crude crashed 11.28% to $87.80/barrel and WTI fell 11.94% to $83.45 after Trump told Republicans the war could end 'pretty quickly.' This was the biggest single-day drop since the conflict began, suggesting markets see de-escalation. However, Netanyahu simultaneously vowed 'many surprises,' revealing a widening gap between US and Israeli war objectives. Only 29% of Americans approve of the strikes (Reuters/Ipsos).
impact: Potential de-escalation signal from the US. But Hormuz remains effectively closed and pump prices lag crude by 1-2 weeks. US-Israel endgames diverging: US wants to wind down, Israel wants to continue. Goldman Sachs still projects CPI could rise to 3% if oil stays elevated.

sources.name: CNN Business
Bloomberg

title: Iran Deploys Super-Heavy Khoramshahr Missiles — Largest Warhead in Arsenal
description: Iran launched its '37th wave' deploying Khoramshahr super-heavy ballistic missiles with 2,000+ km range and 1,800 kg warheads — Iran's most destructive conventional weapon. Targets included Israel, US bases in the Gulf, and Saudi Arabia. The deployment marks a significant escalation in weapons usage.
impact: Iran deploying its heaviest conventional missiles signals increasing desperation or escalatory intent. Interception more challenging due to size. Casualties from debris even when intercepted.

sources.name: Al Jazeera
Times of Israel

title: Lebanon Declares Hezbollah Military Activities Illegal — Historic Break
description: Lebanese cabinet voted 22-2 to declare Hezbollah's military activities illegal — the most significant move against the Iran-backed militia in Lebanon's history. Hezbollah's political wing remains legal. The vote signals a potential break in Iran's proxy network.
impact: Historic rupture in Iran-Hezbollah-Lebanon axis. Could reshape Lebanon's political future and reduce Iranian influence in the Levant. Sets precedent for other host nations of Iran-backed groups.

sources.name: Al Jazeera
ABC News

title: IEA Record 400M Barrel Reserve Release — Largest Emergency Drawdown in History
description: IEA member nations agreed to release 400 million barrels from strategic oil reserves over 60 days — the largest coordinated emergency drawdown in IEA history, surpassing the 2022 release of 182M barrels. Oil rebounded only 3.4%, signaling reserves alone cannot offset the Hormuz blockade.
impact: Unprecedented response to oil supply crisis. Maximum release rate (~6 Mbpd) far less than ~15 Mbpd blocked by Hormuz. Temporary relief only if war continues.

sources.name: CNBC
NPR

title: Iran-Hezbollah Joint 'Integrated Operation' — Coordinated 5-Hour Attack on Israel
description: Iran and Hezbollah launched the first formally coordinated joint attack on Israel — 5 hours of sustained fire striking 50+ targets. Hezbollah fired 150+ rockets at northern Israel while Iran launched ballistic missiles at central Israel simultaneously. IDF responded with large-scale Beirut strikes. This marked a new level of Iran-proxy military integration.
impact: First confirmed Iran-Hezbollah joint military operation against Israel. Establishes precedent for coordinated multi-front attacks. Dramatically raises escalation risk.

sources.name: Haaretz
Jerusalem Post

title: FBI Warns of Iran Drone Threat to US West Coast — Oscars Security Elevated
description: FBI's Joint Terrorism Task Force warned California police that Iran 'aspired to conduct a surprise attack using drones from a vessel off the US coast.' While not deemed credible by officials, the warning marks the first time a direct Iranian military threat to the US homeland has been formally communicated during this war. Oscars security elevated for March 15.
impact: First formal FBI warning of direct Iranian military threat to US homeland. Signals potential asymmetric escalation beyond the Middle East theater.

sources.name: ABC News
The Hill

title: Iran Demands Permanent No-Attack Guarantee + Nuclear Rights as Ceasefire Conditions
description: Iran told mediators it will only accept a ceasefire if the US guarantees neither America nor Israel will ever attack Iran again, plus compensation payments and the right to a full nuclear fuel cycle. Israel's Defense Minister responded operations continue 'without any time limit.' The demands signal negotiations will be extremely difficult.
impact: Iran's maximalist demands make near-term ceasefire unlikely. Nuclear fuel cycle demand particularly explosive given the war began over nuclear program concerns.

sources.name: Bloomberg
Al Jazeera

title: Six Vessels Attacked in Gulf/Hormuz — Total Ships Struck Reaches 16
description: Six commercial vessels attacked in a single day across the Gulf and Strait of Hormuz. Explosive-laden Iranian boats hit two tankers in Iraqi waters (1 killed). Thai ship Mayuree Naree struck (3 crew missing). Japan ship ONE Majesty damaged near UAE. IRGC warned all ships transiting Hormuz will be targeted. US Navy refused escort requests. Iraq shut oil port operations.
impact: Largest single-day attack on shipping since war began. 16 ships now struck total. Iraq oil exports disrupted. Merchant shipping near standstill through Hormuz. Global supply chain effects intensifying.

sources.name: Al Jazeera
AP / Kathmandu Post

title: Iran Publishes Target List of US Tech Giant Offices — 'Infrastructure Warfare' Escalation
description: Iran's Tasnim news agency published detailed target list of US tech company offices in Israel and Gulf states: Amazon, Google, Microsoft, Nvidia, Palantir, IBM, Oracle. Facilities described as 'enemy technology infrastructure.' IRGC also declared US-Israeli 'economic centres and banks' as legitimate targets after Israel struck an Iranian bank.
impact: War expands to economic/technology targets. International bank employees leaving Dubai. Threatens Gulf states' status as tech/finance hubs. Could trigger corporate evacuations and insurance repricing.

sources.name: Times of Israel
The Register

title: First Iran Cyberattack on US Company — Stryker Medical Technology Hacked
description: Pro-Iran hacking group claimed cyberattack on US medical tech company Stryker — the first significant Iran cyberattack on a US company since the war began. Attack framed as retaliation for Minab school strike. US DHS and intelligence community issued flurry of private warnings to American companies.
impact: Opens cyber front against US homeland. Could trigger cascade of retaliatory cyberattacks. Escalates conflict beyond physical battlefield into critical infrastructure domain.

sources.name: NBC News
ABC News

title: Hezbollah-Iran Joint Operation — Largest Combined Barrage on Israel
description: Iran and Hezbollah conducted their largest coordinated attack on Israel: 200+ rockets, 20 drones from Hezbollah plus advanced ballistic missiles from Iran targeting Tel Aviv and Haifa. IRGC described it as a 'joint and integrated operation' lasting 5 hours, claiming 50+ Israeli targets struck. Hundreds of thousands of Israelis sent to shelters.
impact: Largest combined Iran-Hezbollah attack demonstrates increasing coordination and escalation. Tests Israeli air defense capacity. Could trigger Israeli ground invasion of Lebanon if attacks continue.

sources.name: Haaretz
Times of Israel

title: Russia Providing Tactical Drone Advice to Iran — Proxy War Deepens
description: Reports indicate Russia is providing specific tactical drone advice to Iran, helping Tehran hit US and Gulf nation targets. This represents a new level of military support beyond previously reported general assistance, escalating the conflict into a broader proxy war dimension.
impact: Transforms the conflict from a regional war to a proxy confrontation between major powers. Could trigger US response against Russian advisors or assets. Deepens Russia-Iran alliance.

sources.name: Al Jazeera

title: Iraq Oil Ports Shut Down — Energy Crisis Widens Beyond Hormuz
description: Iraq shut down oil port operations after deadly attacks on two foreign tankers. Combined with the Strait of Hormuz blockade, this further restricts global oil supply. Oil surges past $95/bbl. Iran still loading 1.5M bbl/day; China receiving 1.25M bbl/day through Iran's oil lifeline.
impact: Iraq oil port closure adds another chokepoint to global energy supply. Combined with Hormuz blockade, threatens to push oil past $100/bbl again. Iran's continued oil exports to China undermine Western pressure campaign.

sources.name: Al Jazeera

title: Mojtaba Khamenei's First Statement — Vows Hormuz Stays Closed, New Fronts
description: Iran's new Supreme Leader issued his first statement via Telegram, calling for national unity, continued Hormuz closure, and military resistance. He said Iran is studying 'opening of other fronts.' This contradicts President Pezeshkian's peace overtures, revealing a deepening rift in Iranian leadership over war strategy.
impact: New Supreme Leader's hardline first statement signals Iran will not seek peace despite devastation. Threat of 'new fronts' could mean activating Houthis, expanding to Gulf states, or cyber attacks. Leadership rift between Mojtaba and Pezeshkian creates unpredictability.

sources.name: Al Jazeera
CNBC

title: Brent Crude Crosses $100 — IEA Emergency Reserve Release
description: Brent crude surged past $100/barrel for the first time since 2022 (+9.2% on the day). The IEA agreed to release a record 400 million barrels from emergency reserves, with the US contributing 172 million from the SPR. US stock markets hit 2026 closing lows. Rystad Energy warned Brent could hit $135 if the situation persists 4 months.
impact: Oil crossing $100 triggers emergency reserve releases not seen since Russia-Ukraine 2022. $135 Brent would push US gas past $5/gallon nationally. SPR drawdown reduces US strategic buffer. Economic pressure mounting on all parties.

sources.name: CNN
US News

title: Israel Expands Lebanon Operations Past Litani — Strikes Evacuation Zone
description: Israel expanded military operations past the Litani River to the Zahrani River with new evacuation orders. Overnight strikes hit Beirut's Corniche seafront — a designated evacuation area — killing 8+. Hezbollah retook positions south of the Litani. Lebanon front toll now 687 killed, 800,000 displaced.
impact: Expansion past Litani into Zahrani area significantly widens the Lebanon front. Strikes on designated evacuation zones draw international condemnation. Hezbollah retaking positions signals the Nov 2024 ceasefire has fully collapsed.

sources.name: ABC News
NPR

title: KC-135 Tanker Crash — 4th US Aircraft Lost in Conflict
description: A US Air Force KC-135 Stratotanker crashed during operations related to the Iran campaign, killing all crew members. This marks the 4th US aircraft lost since the war began. The crash raises questions about operational tempo and the strain on aging refueling fleet assets critical to sustained air operations over Iran.
impact: 4th US aircraft loss deepens concerns about operational sustainability. KC-135 fleet is critical for refueling combat aircraft over Iran — loss strains already-stretched tanker capacity. Congressional scrutiny of air campaign safety increasing.

sources.name: CBS News

title: France Suffers First Military Casualty — NATO Expansion Fears
description: A French soldier was killed in an Iraqi militia drone attack on a French counterterrorism base in Iraq, marking France's first military fatality in the conflict. President Macron called the death 'unacceptable' and demanded accountability. The incident raises fears of NATO being drawn further into the war.
impact: First NATO member casualty outside the US-Israel coalition. France's counterterrorism presence in Iraq now directly affected by Iran-aligned militia operations. Macron's strong response could trigger further European involvement or withdrawal debate.

sources.name: France 24

title: Iran Launches Most Intense Missile Operation of the War
description: Iran launched its most intense missile operation of the conflict, deploying advanced ballistic missiles including Khoramshahr variants in coordinated salvos. The operation targeted Israeli military installations and US regional bases simultaneously, testing the limits of multi-layered air defense systems.
impact: Iran's most intense single-day missile operation demonstrates retained offensive capability despite 13 days of sustained US-Israeli strikes. Tests the endurance of Arrow, David's Sling, and Patriot interceptor stockpiles.

sources.name: Al Jazeera

title: Black Rain Environmental Crisis Spreads Across Region
description: Reports of 'black rain' — oil-contaminated rainfall from burning petroleum infrastructure — spreading across parts of Iran, Iraq, and Kuwait. The environmental crisis stems from sustained strikes on oil refineries and storage facilities. Health authorities warn of respiratory hazards and water contamination.
impact: Environmental disaster reminiscent of 1991 Gulf War oil fires. Long-term health and ecological consequences for civilian populations. Complicates humanitarian access and refugee movements. Water contamination threatens already-strained civilian infrastructure.

sources.name: CBS News

title: NATO Intercepts Third Iranian Missile Over Turkey
description: NATO air defenses intercepted a third Iranian ballistic missile over Turkish territory near Adana at 3:25 AM local time. Turkey confirmed the intercept, marking the third time Iranian missiles have entered NATO member airspace during the conflict.
impact: Third missile intercept over NATO territory raises the specter of Article 5 invocation. Turkey's position between Iran and Israel makes it increasingly vulnerable. NATO alliance cohesion tested as intercepts become routine.

sources.name: Al Jazeera

title: Iranian Cluster Missiles Penetrate Israeli Air Defenses
description: 11 Iranian cluster missiles penetrated Israel's multi-layered air defense system. One missile dropped approximately 70 bomblets over central Israel, killing 2 Israelis and wounding 50+ in a strike on Zarzir in northern Israel. The cluster munition tactic represents a significant evolution in Iran's ability to bypass interceptors.
impact: Demonstrates Iran's ability to penetrate Israeli air defenses with cluster warheads. 50+ wounded in single strike. Raises questions about interceptor stockpile sustainability against saturating cluster attacks.

sources.name: Haaretz
Haaretz Day 14 Liveblog

title: Michigan Synagogue Attack — Domestic Blowback From War
description: A synagogue in Michigan was attacked amid rising tensions linked to the Iran-Israel war. The incident highlights domestic security spillover from the overseas conflict, with antisemitic incidents surging in the US since the war began.
impact: Domestic terrorism linked to overseas conflict. FBI reportedly investigating as hate crime. Jewish communities across the US on heightened alert.

sources.name: CNN

title: Explosion at Tehran Al-Quds Day Rally Kills One
description: An explosion struck an Al-Quds Day solidarity march in Tehran, killing at least 1 person and injuring several others. The cause remains unclear — Iranian authorities have not confirmed whether it was a targeted attack or accidental detonation.
impact: Explosion at a major public gathering raises questions about internal security during wartime. Al-Quds Day rallies are significant political events in Iran. Could rally further support for the war effort or expose regime vulnerability.

sources.name: Al Jazeera

title: 2,200 Marines Deployed — Ground Operations Not Ruled Out
description: 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit with 2,200 Marines ordered from Japan to Middle East on 3 warships with ~20 F-35Bs. Pentagon refuses to rule out ground operations to secure Iran's enriched uranium. Represents potential shift from air campaign to ground intervention.
impact: Potential ground invasion of Iran would dramatically escalate the conflict. MEU deployment suggests Pentagon is preparing for ground operations as nuclear facility security becomes primary objective.

sources.name: Axios
Military Times

title: Israel Destroys Civilian Bridge in Lebanon — Threatens 'Gaza-Scale Devastation'
description: Israel destroys the Zrarieh Bridge over the Litani River — first acknowledged strike on civilian infrastructure. Leaflets dropped over Beirut warning of 'Gaza-scale devastation.' Expansion to areas never previously targeted.
impact: Targeting of civilian infrastructure marks a significant escalation in the Lebanon front. 'Gaza-scale devastation' warning signals potential total war in Lebanon. 800,000+ already displaced.

sources.name: Al Jazeera
Washington Post

title: US Waives Russian Oil Sanctions — Geopolitical Realignment
description: US Treasury waives Russian oil sanctions for 30 days to ease supply crunch from Hormuz blockade. EU condemns the move. The war is forcing unprecedented geopolitical shifts — the US is now effectively facilitating Russian oil trade to contain energy crisis.
impact: Undermines Western sanctions regime against Russia. Iran war forcing US to choose between energy security and anti-Russia policy. Could embolden Russia to increase Ukraine operations.

sources.name: Washington Post
Irish Times

title: Iran Considers Yuan-Only Hormuz Access — Dollar Dominance Challenged
description: Iran reportedly considering allowing tankers through Hormuz only if trade conducted in Chinese yuan. If implemented, would represent the most significant challenge to US dollar dominance in global oil markets since the petrodollar system was established in the 1970s.
impact: Could reshape global oil trade if implemented. China gains significant leverage. Yuan-denominated oil trade would weaken dollar's role as reserve currency.

sources.name: Bloomberg

title: Kharg Island Massive Bombing Raid — Oil Export Hub Hit
description: CENTCOM strikes all military targets on Kharg Island, Iran's primary oil export hub handling 90% of crude exports. 15+ explosions destroy army defenses, Joshen Sea Base, airport control tower, helicopter hangar. Oil infrastructure deliberately spared but Trump warns it's next if Hormuz remains blocked.
impact: First strikes on Kharg Island — signals willingness to threaten Iran's economic lifeline. Oil markets react with Brent firmly above $100. Iran threatens to strike oil companies with US connections in retaliation.

sources.name: The Hill
CNBC

title: US Embassy Baghdad Struck by Missile — IRGC Claims Responsibility
description: Missile strikes helipad inside US Embassy compound in Baghdad after a suicide drone first targeted the radar system. IRGC confirms this as the '48th phase' of its retaliatory campaign. First direct hit on US Embassy in Baghdad since the war began.
impact: Direct attack on US diplomatic compound represents major escalation by Iran/proxies. Raises questions about embassy security and potential US retaliatory action against Iraq-based militia positions.

sources.name: Jerusalem Post
Al Jazeera

title: NATO Intercepts 3rd Iranian Missile Over Turkey — Alliance Tensions Rise
description: Turkey reports NATO defenses intercepted a third Iranian missile in Turkish airspace. Adana residents near Incirlik airbase woken by sirens at 3:25 AM. Repeated violations of NATO member airspace could trigger Article 5 discussions.
impact: Third NATO interception creates pathway to broader alliance involvement. If Article 5 is invoked, the war could expand dramatically. Turkey caught between NATO obligations and maintaining relations with Iran.

sources.name: Al Jazeera
Bloomberg

title: $10M Bounty on Iran's Supreme Leader — Unprecedented Targeting
description: State Department announces $10 million reward for information on Mojtaba Khamenei and 7 senior IRGC commanders. The bounty on a sitting head of state is unprecedented and signals US intent to pursue regime decapitation.
impact: Bounty on sitting head of state escalates personal targeting beyond military operations. Could incentivize internal Iranian actors to cooperate with US intelligence. Iran likely to view this as an existential threat.

sources.name: Israel Hayom
Washington Examiner

title: IRGC Threatens UAE Cities — First Direct Threat to Gulf Ally Civilians
description: IRGC warns residents of UAE cities to evacuate areas near ports and docks hosting US forces. Calls American positions 'legitimate targets.' First time Iran has directly threatened a Gulf ally's civilian population, marking a significant regional escalation beyond the Iran-Israel theater.
impact: Could trigger broader Gulf conflict. UAE has already absorbed 1,668 strikes (1,422 drones + 246 missiles). If Iranian attacks on UAE cities intensify, other Gulf states may escalate involvement or the conflict could draw in additional parties.

sources.name: The Hill

title: Fujairah Port Attacked — Last Alternative to Hormuz Disrupted
description: Drone attack and fire at Fujairah port (UAE) forces suspension of oil loading operations. Fujairah was the primary alternative route for oil exports bypassing the Strait of Hormuz. Its disruption further tightens the global oil supply bottleneck.
impact: With both Hormuz effectively closed and Fujairah disrupted, there are virtually no routes for Persian Gulf oil exports. Could push Brent well above $110-120 if sustained. Global energy crisis deepens.

sources.name: Gulf News

title: KC-135 Crash Kills 6 US Service Members in Iraq
description: US KC-135 refueling aircraft crashes in western Iraq killing all 6 crew. US military deaths now at 13 total. CENTCOM rules out hostile fire but Iranian proxy claims responsibility.
impact: Raises US military casualty toll significantly. Three crew from Ohio ANG 121st Air Refueling Wing.

sources.name: NBC News

title: Friendly Fire Downs 3 US F-15E Strike Eagles Over Kuwait
description: Three F-15E Strike Eagles shot down in mistaken friendly fire over Kuwait. All 6 crew ejected safely. Worst friendly fire aviation incident since 2003 Iraq War.
impact: Exposes chaos and coordination failures in multi-front air campaign. Pentagon investigating.

sources.name: CNN

title: Trump Rejects All Ceasefire Efforts
description: Trump administration rebuffs ceasefire mediation by Oman and Egypt. Senior official says 'He's not interested in that right now.' Trump tells G7 Iran is 'about to surrender.'
impact: Eliminates near-term diplomatic off-ramp. War expected to continue without negotiations.

sources.name: Al-Monitor

title: Israel Plans Major Ground Invasion of Lebanon
description: Israel planning massive ground operation south of Litani River targeting Hezbollah infrastructure. Lebanese government outlaws Hezbollah military wing and pledges IRGC arrests.
impact: Opens potential new ground front. Risks wider regional escalation and significant civilian casualties in Lebanon.

sources.name: Axios

title: IEA Authorizes Largest-Ever 400M Barrel Reserve Release
description: International Energy Agency authorizes record 400M barrel coordinated release. US contributing 172M from SPR. Despite release, Brent remains above $100/barrel.
impact: Unprecedented emergency measure fails to bring oil below $100. Signals severity of supply disruption from Hormuz closure.

sources.name: IEA

title: US Strikes 90 Military Targets on Kharg Island
description: CENTCOM strikes 90 military targets on Kharg Island — Iran's critical oil export hub handling 90% of crude exports. Oil infrastructure deliberately spared. Trump warns 'immediate reconsideration' if Hormuz blocked.
impact: Striking Kharg Island sends clear escalatory signal — oil infrastructure is next if Iran doesn't comply. Iran's primary export capability under direct threat.

sources.name: Washington Post
Al Jazeera

title: Iran Retaliates — Missiles Hit Israel and Gulf States
description: Iran launches retaliatory missiles at central Israel, Eilat (child injured), and Jerusalem. Claims Khaibar missiles hit Netanyahu's office — unconfirmed. Qatar intercepts 4 ballistic missiles. Kuwait base hit by drones. UAE Fujairah fire from drone debris.
impact: Iran continues retaliating despite 90% missile capability reduction. Gulf states increasingly drawn into direct conflict.

sources.name: Al Jazeera
CNN

title: Iran-Linked Cyberattack Hits Medical Giant Stryker
description: Iran-linked 'Handala' group launches major cyberattack wiping Stryker employee phones worldwide. CISA launches investigation. 60+ hacktivist groups now active. Poland reports Iranian hack attempt on nuclear research center.
impact: Iran opens cyber front targeting Western corporate infrastructure. Medical device company attack could affect healthcare supply chains.

sources.name: Al Jazeera
Axios

title: IRGC Launches 10 Hypersonic Missiles at UAE Air Base
description: Iran's IRGC claims direct attack on US forces at Al Dhafra Air Base in UAE with 10 missiles and drones, marking continued escalation of attacks on Gulf state hosting US military.
impact: Direct Iranian missile attack on US forces in UAE. Raises risk of broader Gulf state involvement.

sources.name: CNBC
Al Jazeera

title: IRGC Issues Direct Death Threat Against Netanyahu
description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards publicly vow to 'pursue and kill child-killer Netanyahu if he is still alive.' The explicit assassination threat against a sitting head of state marks a new level of escalatory rhetoric.
impact: Unprecedented public assassination threat against Israeli PM. Raises security concerns for Israeli leadership.

sources.name: CNBC

title: Brent Crude Closes Above $100 for Second Straight Day
description: Oil prices remain elevated with Brent at $103.14 and WTI at $98.71 as IEA's 400M barrel reserve release fails to stem prices. Gas reaches $3.68/gallon nationally. Gold hits $5,032/oz.
impact: Sustained $100+ oil threatens global recession. Goldman raises US recession odds to 25%.

sources.name: Trading Economics
AAA Gas Prices

title: F-35I vs Yak-130: First stealth-vs-manned aerial combat in history
description: An Israeli F-35I Adir engaged and shot down an Iranian Yak-130 in what is believed to be the first-ever aerial combat between a stealth fighter and a manned adversary aircraft. The engagement marks a historic milestone in military aviation.
impact: Historic military aviation milestone — first confirmed engagement between a stealth fighter and manned adversary aircraft

sources.name: Al Jazeera

title: US submarine sinks Iranian warship IRIS Dena — 87 killed
description: A US Navy submarine torpedoed and sank the Iranian warship IRIS Dena, killing 87 crew members. The attack is the first submarine sinking of a warship since World War II, marking a major escalation in naval warfare.
impact: First submarine attack on a warship since WWII — major escalation in naval warfare

sources.name: Al Jazeera

title: Israel reportedly 'running critically low' on missile interceptors
description: US officials report that Israel is running critically low on missile interceptors after sustained Iranian ballistic missile barrages over 16 days of conflict. The shortage raises serious questions about Israel's ability to defend against continued Iranian attacks.
impact: If confirmed, limits Israel's ability to defend against Iranian ballistic missile barrages

sources.name: Semafor

title: IDF launches 'wide-scale' western Iran strikes — 200+ targets, senior commanders killed
description: Israel launches over 20 waves of strikes hitting 200+ targets across western Iran. IRGC commanders Jalali-Nasab and Shariat confirmed killed. Described as one of the largest single-day strike operations of the war.
impact: Massive escalation in strike tempo. Two more senior IRGC commanders eliminated, further degrading Iranian command structure.

sources.name: Jerusalem Post

title: Iran cluster munition MIRV strike on Bnei Brak — split-warhead first use
description: Iran fires a cluster munition MIRV (multiple independently targetable reentry vehicle) at Bnei Brak in central Israel, marking the first confirmed use of split-warhead technology in the conflict. The strike demonstrates advancing Iranian missile capabilities.
impact: First use of MIRV cluster munitions by Iran. Demonstrates capability to overwhelm missile defenses with split warheads.

sources.name: Jerusalem Post

title: Baghdad Airport rocket attack — 5 wounded, US Embassy security alert
description: Rockets strike Baghdad International Airport, wounding at least 5 people. The US Embassy issues a security alert. The attack highlights the expanding proxy dimension of the conflict in Iraq.
impact: Continued proxy attacks on Iraqi infrastructure. US Embassy security alert signals growing threat to American personnel.

sources.name: Middle East Eye

title: Italian MQ-9 Reaper destroyed at Ali Al Salem — first NATO ally hardware loss
description: An Italian MQ-9 Reaper drone is destroyed at Ali Al Salem Air Base in Kuwait, marking the first confirmed loss of a NATO ally's military hardware in the conflict. Italy confirms no casualties.
impact: First NATO ally (non-US) military hardware destroyed. Raises questions about alliance exposure and potential Article 5 implications.

sources.name: Daily Sabah

title: Iran threatens to strike specific neighborhoods in Dubai and Doha
description: Iran issues explicit warnings naming specific neighborhoods in Dubai and Doha as potential strike targets, triggering evacuation warnings in both cities. The threats mark an unprecedented direct targeting of Gulf civilian areas.
impact: Direct threats against specific civilian areas in allied Gulf states. Evacuation warnings issued. Major escalation in regional threat level.

sources.name: Times of Israel

title: $10M bounty on Mojtaba Khamenei and 9 IRGC officials
description: The US State Department offers a $10 million reward for information leading to the capture of Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei and 9 senior IRGC officials. The bounty program represents an unprecedented targeting of a sitting head of state.
impact: Unprecedented US bounty on a sitting head of state. Signals regime change intent and personal targeting of Iranian leadership.

sources.name: CBS News

title: IDF Ground Operation in Lebanon — Enters Khiam, Largest Since 2006
description: Israeli military launches ground operations in southern Lebanon, entering the town of Khiam in the largest IDF ground incursion into Lebanon since the 2006 war. The operation marks a major escalation of the Lebanon front beyond airstrikes.
impact: First major IDF ground operation in Lebanon since 2006. Significantly escalates the Lebanon front and risks broader regional ground war.

sources.name: Al Jazeera

title: Dubai Airport Fuel Tank Fire from Iranian Drone — Flights Suspended 7+ Hours
description: An Iranian drone strike ignites a fuel tank fire at Dubai International Airport (DXB), one of the world's busiest aviation hubs. All flights suspended for over 7 hours, stranding thousands of passengers and disrupting global air travel.
impact: Attack on major civilian aviation hub. Dubai airport handles 90M+ passengers annually. Sends shockwave through global aviation and insurance markets.

sources.name: Washington Post

title: IRGC Declares USS Gerald Ford Support Network as 'Legitimate Targets'
description: The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps officially declares the entire USS Gerald R. Ford carrier strike group support network — including supply ships, tankers, and allied naval vessels — as 'legitimate military targets.' The declaration expands Iran's stated targeting doctrine beyond direct combatants.
impact: Dramatically expands Iran's declared target set. Threatens logistics and support vessels, potentially including allied nation ships. Raises risk of attacks on non-combatant vessels.

sources.name: Press TV

title: Fujairah Oil Hub Struck Again — 2nd Consecutive Day of Attacks
description: Iran strikes the UAE's Fujairah oil terminal and storage facility for the second consecutive day, causing additional fires and disruptions. Fujairah is one of the world's largest oil bunkering ports and a critical bypass for Strait of Hormuz shipping.
impact: Repeated strikes on critical oil infrastructure. Fujairah port handles ~3.5M barrels/day of oil storage. Insurance premiums for Gulf shipping spike further.

sources.name: CNBC

title: Trump Threatens NATO Allies, Weighs Kharg Island Seizure
description: President Trump threatens NATO allies over their perceived non-response to Strait of Hormuz disruptions, and publicly weighs seizing Iran's Kharg Island — which handles ~90% of Iran's oil exports — as an 'economic knockout blow.' The statements alarm European allies and raise fears of further escalation.
impact: Threatens transatlantic alliance cohesion. Kharg Island seizure would be a massive military escalation requiring amphibious operations. Markets react to expanded war fears.

sources.name: NPR

title: Russian Consulate Closes in Isfahan After Deadly Weekend Attacks
description: Russia closes its consulate in Isfahan, Iran's third-largest city, citing security concerns after deadly weekend strikes. The closure signals even Russia — Iran's nominal ally — is distancing itself from the escalating conflict zone.
impact: Russia distancing from Iran despite alliance. Signals Isfahan is considered too dangerous even for diplomatic presence. Undermines Iran's claim of Russian support.

sources.name: The Moscow Times

title: Zero Commercial Transits Through Strait of Hormuz
description: For the first time in history, zero commercial vessel transits recorded through Strait of Hormuz on March 14. IEA calls it 'largest supply disruption in history.'
impact: Complete maritime trade stoppage through world's most important oil chokepoint. 21% of global oil supply affected.

sources.name: Windward Maritime Intelligence

title: Iranian Missile Fragments Near Jerusalem Holy Sites and Knesset
description: Fragments from intercepted Iranian missiles fell near Church of the Holy Sepulchre, National Library near Knesset, and East Jerusalem.
impact: Missile debris near Christianity's holiest site and Israel's parliament. 108 Israelis hospitalized in past 24 hours.

sources.name: Times of Israel

title: All Major Allies Decline Hormuz Coalition — Trump Threatens NATO
description: UK, Germany, Australia, Japan all declined Trump's request to send warships to Strait of Hormuz. No country has publicly committed.
impact: Unprecedented diplomatic isolation for US war effort.

sources.name: National Today
CNN

title: Iran Reveals Russia-China Military Cooperation
description: FM Araghchi disclosed Russia and China are providing 'military cooperation' to Iran. First official confirmation of direct military support from major powers.
impact: Transforms conflict dynamics. Could lead to broader great-power confrontation.

sources.name: Al Jazeera
CBS News

title: IDF Launches Ground Operation in Southern Lebanon — Takes Khiam
description: IDF 91st Division launched ground operations in southern Lebanon with Merkava tanks and APCs. Took strategic town of Khiam. 450,000 reservists called up. Marks significant expansion of conflict into ground war.
impact: Opens ground war front in Lebanon. 1M+ displaced. G5 nations warned against 'devastating humanitarian consequences.' IDF plans 3+ more weeks of operations.

sources.name: Al Jazeera
Times of Israel

title: Fujairah Oil Terminal Hit Again — UAE's Non-Hormuz Export Route Suspended
description: Iran struck Fujairah Oil Zone for second time in two days. ADNOC suspended loading at UAE's only oil export route outside Hormuz. Handles up to 1.8M bbl/day. Closes off last remaining Gulf oil bypass.
impact: With both Hormuz and Fujairah disrupted, virtually all Gulf oil exports are now compromised. Brent spiked to $106.50.

sources.name: CNBC
Gulf News

title: Iran Uses Cluster Munitions Against Israeli Civilian Areas
description: Iran fired Fajr-type missiles with cluster munition warheads at central Israel (Rishon Lezion, Lod, Shoham). Cluster munitions are banned under international law (Convention on Cluster Munitions). 10 locations damaged.
impact: First confirmed use of cluster munitions in the conflict. Banned by 123 countries. Could trigger additional international condemnation of Iran.

sources.name: Jerusalem Post
Ynet

title: UAE Closes Entire National Airspace Amid Escalating Iranian Strikes
description: UAE closes all national airspace after Iranian drones struck Dubai airport fuel tanks and Fujairah oil hub on consecutive days. All flights suspended at DXB, the world's busiest international airport.
impact: Global aviation disrupted. Thousands of flights diverted. Dubai airport — world's busiest international hub — shut down indefinitely.

sources.name: US News
Al Jazeera

title: Israel Strikes Shah Gas Field — Iran's Largest Natural Gas Reserve
description: Israeli jets struck Iran's Shah gas field (South Pars complex), the world's largest natural gas field shared with Qatar. Major fire reported. Could affect global LNG supply if damage is severe.
impact: Threatens global natural gas supply. Shah/South Pars supplies 40% of Iran's domestic gas and is shared with Qatar. LNG prices spike.

sources.name: US News
Gulf News

title: Bessent Says US Will Allow Iranian Tankers Through Hormuz — Oil Drops 4-6%
description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent announced the US would allow Iranian oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz. Oil prices immediately dropped: Brent -4.3% to $100.21, WTI -6.1% to $93.50. Signals potential de-escalation on economic front.
impact: Oil drops sharply. First signal of potential Hormuz de-escalation. Markets rally on hopes of reduced economic disruption.

sources.name: CNBC
Washington Examiner

title: IRGC Commander al-Askari Killed in Green Zone Drone Strike
description: Senior IRGC Quds Force commander Brig. Gen. Hossein al-Askari killed in drone strike on Baghdad's Green Zone. Al-Askari was coordinating Iran-backed militia operations across Iraq.
impact: Major blow to IRGC command structure in Iraq. Could disrupt Iran-backed militia coordination. Risk of retaliatory attacks on US forces in Iraq.

sources.name: Al Jazeera
Jerusalem Post

title: Israel Claims Killing Ali Larijani — Iran's De Facto Leader
description: Defense Minister Katz announced Ali Larijani, secretary of Supreme National Security Council and de facto ruler since Khamenei's death, killed in overnight strikes. Biggest targeted killing since Khamenei. Iran has not confirmed.
impact: If confirmed, removes Iran's most senior decision-maker. Could trigger leadership vacuum or more extreme IRGC response. Escalation risk very high.

sources.name: Al Jazeera
Washington Post

title: Israel Ground Invasion of Southern Lebanon — 100,000 Troops Deployed
description: Israel launched ground operations south of Litani River with 6 divisions (~100,000 soldiers). Defense Minister compared to Gaza war and suggested indefinite occupation. 886 killed in Lebanon, 1M displaced.
impact: Opens major new front. Risks direct confrontation with Hezbollah ground forces. Five Western nations issued joint statement calling for invasion to 'be averted'.

sources.name: Al Jazeera
Axios

title: Iran Cluster Munitions Hit Central Israel — Train Station Damaged
description: Fourth ballistic missile salvo with cluster warheads struck central Israel. Damage at train station; fragments near Knesset and Church of Holy Sepulchre. ~300 missiles fired at Israel in war, half carry cluster warheads.
impact: Cluster munitions banned by most nations. Demonstrates Iran's continued ability to hit Israeli population centers despite air defenses.

sources.name: Times of Israel
Haaretz

title: Most Intense Attack on US Embassy Baghdad — Drone Penetrates Compound
description: 3 drones + 4 rockets fired at US Embassy Green Zone. Most intense since war began. C-RAM intercepted 2, 1 drone struck inside compound. Al-Rasheed Hotel also hit. 4 killed in US retaliatory strike.
impact: Escalates Iraq front. Drone penetrating embassy compound represents security failure. Could trigger expanded US operations against Iraqi militias.

sources.name: Al Jazeera
CNN

title: Second Fujairah Oil Zone Drone Strike — UAE's Last Export Bypass Threatened
description: Fresh drone strike caused fire at Fujairah Oil Industry Zone, second attack in 3 days. ADNOC loading halted. Threatens to sever UAE's last crude export route bypassing Hormuz.
impact: If Fujairah export capacity eliminated, UAE loses ability to export oil without transiting Hormuz. Major blow to global energy supply chain.

sources.name: CNBC
Arabian Business

title: Joe Kent Resigns as NCTC Director — First Senior Official to Quit Over War
description: National Counterterrorism Center Director Joe Kent resigned, calling Iran 'no imminent threat' and blaming 'pressure from Israel.' Highest-profile Trump administration resignation over the war. His claim directly contradicts the Article II legal justification.
impact: Major political blow to war justification; Congressional subpoena likely; undermines 'imminent threat' legal basis

sources.name: NPR
Axios

title: Iran Deploys Haj Qasem Missile — First Combat Use of Advanced MaRV System
description: IRGC used the Haj Qasem ballistic missile for the first time — 1,400km range with maneuverable reentry vehicle designed to defeat THAAD and Patriot systems. Named after Qasem Soleimani. Targeted Israeli cities and US bases across the Gulf.
impact: Significant escalation in missile capability; MaRV technology threatens to overwhelm air defense systems; 59th attack wave

sources.name: EADaily

title: Missile Fragments Hit Jerusalem Holy Sites — Temple Mount, Holy Sepulchre
description: Missile and interceptor fragments found at Temple Mount/Al-Aqsa Mosque, Church of the Holy Sepulchre, Armenian Patriarchate, and Jewish Quarter. A warhead landed less than 1km from Temple Mount. Risk of global religious escalation.
impact: Potential flashpoint for global religious tensions; damage to sites sacred to Islam, Christianity, and Judaism

sources.name: Times of Israel

title: Trump Threatens to Leave NATO Over Hormuz Refusal
description: Trump said leaving NATO is 'something to think about' after every major ally refused to join Hormuz escort coalition. Germany, Japan, Italy, Australia confirmed refusal; UK, France noncommittal. Deepest NATO rift in decades.
impact: Most severe NATO crisis since alliance founding; allies fear being drawn into US-initiated war; transatlantic security framework shaken

sources.name: Time
Axios

title: Iran Strikes UAE Shah Gas Field — 20% of UAE Gas Supply Ablaze
description: Iran attacked UAE's Shah gas field, setting it ablaze. The field supplies 20% of UAE natural gas. Fujairah oil terminal hit for the 3rd time in 4 days. Oil prices surged 3%+ to $103.50/barrel Brent on the attack.
impact: Direct threat to UAE energy security; oil surges; Fujairah — UAE's Hormuz bypass route — increasingly untenable

sources.name: CNBC

title: Russia Sharing Satellite Imagery and Drone Tech with Iran — WSJ
description: WSJ reports Russia expanding intelligence sharing with Iran — satellite imagery of US force locations and improved Shahed drone components using Ukraine warfare experience. Iran's targeting accuracy against US assets has improved.
impact: Russia effectively becoming co-belligerent; improved Iranian drone/missile targeting threatens US force safety

sources.name: US News / WSJ

title: Projectile Strikes Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant Premises
description: A projectile hit premises of Iran's Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant near the operating unit. IAEA confirmed no reactor damage. Rosatom condemned the strike. Russia evacuating personnel. Most dangerous nuclear escalation of the war.
impact: First strike near an active nuclear reactor; risks nuclear contamination; Russia furious; major international red line approached

sources.name: US News

title: US Embassy Baghdad Hit by Coordinated Drone and Rocket Attack
description: 3 explosive drones and rockets targeted US Embassy in Baghdad's Green Zone. 1 drone struck outer wall. C-RAM defenses activated near Baghdad airport. 2nd consecutive day of embassy attacks.
impact: Direct attack on US diplomatic compound; Iraq front rapidly escalating; militia proxies increasingly bold

sources.name: Al Jazeera

title: IRGC 'Rapid Strike' Retaliation Kills 2 in Ramat Gan
description: IRGC launched Khorramshahr-4 and Qadr multiple-warhead missiles in retaliation for Larijani killing. Elderly couple killed in Ramat Gan. Shrapnel hit Tel Aviv train station. 5 wounded.
impact: Named retaliation for political assassination; new missile types used; civilian casualties in Tel Aviv metro area

sources.name: Haaretz

title: Israel Kills Intelligence Minister Khatib — Third Assassination in 48h
description: IDF airstrike killed Iran's Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib, third senior official eliminated after Larijani and Soleimani. Katz announced all Iranian officials now authorized targets.
impact: Systematic decapitation of Iranian political and intelligence leadership; unprecedented targeted killing campaign

sources.name: Axios

title: South Pars Gas Field Struck — First Upstream Energy Attack
description: Israeli jets hit South Pars/Assaluyeh — world's largest gas field. Two refineries halted. First strike on upstream energy infrastructure. Brent surged 5%.
impact: Energy war escalation; Iran threatens retaliation against Gulf energy sites; global energy crisis deepening

sources.name: CTV News/AP

title: Iran Threatens Gulf Energy Infrastructure as Legitimate Targets
description: Iran named specific Gulf energy facilities as legitimate targets: Saudi Samref, Jubail, UAE Al Hasan, Qatar plants. Major escalation risk to global energy supply.
impact: If carried out, would remove ~25% of global oil supply from market; could trigger global economic crisis

sources.name: Times of Israel

title: US Drops Bunker Busters on Hormuz — Campaign to Reopen Strait
description: CENTCOM used 5,000-lb deep penetrator munitions against hardened Iranian antiship missile sites along Hormuz coastline. Opening of multi-week campaign to reopen waterway.
impact: Direct military campaign to break Hormuz blockade; major naval escalation

sources.name: NBC News

title: Iran Strikes Qatar's Ras Laffan LNG — World's Largest
description: Iranian missiles struck Ras Laffan Industrial City, causing extensive damage to world's largest LNG export terminal (~20% of global LNG). Qatar expelled Iran's military attaches. War now directly hitting allied Gulf states' energy infrastructure.
impact: First Iranian strike on ally's energy infrastructure; shatters Gulf neutrality; threatens 20% of global LNG supply

sources.name: Al Jazeera

title: Gabbard Testifies Iran NOT Rebuilding Enrichment — War Justification Undercut
description: DNI Gabbard omitted prepared statement that Iran had NOT restarted uranium enrichment before war. IAEA reveals new underground site at Isfahan never inspected. Major political crisis for war justification.
impact: Directly undercuts administration's stated justification for war; political fallout expected

sources.name: Washington Post

title: Iran Fires Missiles at Saudi Arabia, UAE, Qatar in Single Day
description: Iran simultaneously attacked energy/military sites in UAE (13 missiles + 27 drones), Saudi Arabia (4 missiles + 21 drones), and Qatar (Ras Laffan). Broadest geographic attack of war — conflict now fully regional.
impact: War now a regional conflict; every major Gulf state under direct Iranian fire

sources.name: Gulf News

title: Ras Laffan Struck Second Time — World's Largest LNG Under Sustained Attack
description: Iran hit Qatar's Ras Laffan a second time in 12 hours. Sizeable fires and extensive damage.
impact: 20% of global LNG supply offline; sustained attacks on Gulf energy infrastructure

sources.name: Times of Israel

title: Saudi Arabia Reserves Right to Take Military Action Against Iran
description: Saudi FM declared trust in Iran completely shattered. 12-nation joint statement demands Iran halt attacks.
impact: Saudi entering military posture; Gulf coalition forming; war could expand to direct Saudi-Iran conflict

sources.name: Al Jazeera

title: Israel Strikes South Pars Gas Field — Conflict Enters Energy War Phase
description: Israeli Air Force strikes four gas treatment facilities at Iran's South Pars gas field in Bushehr Province, coordinated with the US. South Pars accounts for 36% of Iran's gas reserves. Iran threatens retaliatory strikes on all Gulf energy infrastructure.
impact: War transitions from military to energy infrastructure targeting. Global gas prices surge 30%+. Iraq loses Iranian gas supply.

sources.name: Bloomberg
NPR

title: Iran Retaliates on ALL 6 Gulf States' Energy Infrastructure
description: Iran attacks energy facilities across all six GCC countries simultaneously for the first time in history: Qatar Ras Laffan, Saudi SAMREF, Kuwait refineries, UAE Habshan, plus Bahrain and Oman targets. Shell Pearl GTL plant damaged.
impact: Unprecedented regional escalation. Brent surges past $119 intraday. All Gulf energy output at risk. Qatar expels Iranian attaches.

sources.name: Al Jazeera
Military.com

title: IDF First-Ever Caspian Sea Strike — Targets Iran-Russia Supply Lifeline
description: IDF Air Force strikes Iranian Navy vessels at Bandar Anzali on the Caspian Sea, the first Israeli military operation in northern Iran. Destroys 4 missile boats and a corvette, targeting the Iran-Russia supply corridor.
impact: War extends to Iran's northern border, threatening the Russia-Iran supply route. Deepens Russia's diplomatic dilemma over the conflict.

sources.name: Israel Hayom

title: Pentagon Requests $200 Billion War Supplemental
description: Pentagon submits $200+ billion supplemental budget request for the Iran war, the largest military supplemental since Iraq/Afghanistan. Defense Secretary Hegseth confirms the request, saying 'it takes money to kill bad guys.'
impact: Signals US preparing for extended conflict. Combined with existing $1T defense budget, US military spending approaches $1.2T annualized.

sources.name: Washington Post

title: Third Assassination in 48 Hours — Intelligence Minister Khatib Killed
description: Israel assassinates Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib overnight, the third senior Iranian official killed in 48 hours after Larijani and IRGC Aerospace chief. Systematic decapitation of Iranian leadership continues.
impact: Iran's intelligence apparatus decapitated. Deepens internal chaos and succession crisis. Fuels Iranian calls for maximum retaliation.

sources.name: Al Jazeera

title: First Hostile Hit on F-35 in History
description: USAF F-35 struck by suspected Iranian fire during combat mission, forced emergency landing. First confirmed hostile damage to the F-35 stealth fighter. If Iran can track and hit F-35s, it fundamentally challenges US air superiority assumptions.
impact: First proof that advanced air defenses can damage stealth fighters in combat. May force changes to US air campaign tactics.

sources.name: CNN
The Aviationist

title: UNSC Resolution 2817 Condemns Iran's Gulf Attacks
description: UN Security Council adopted Resolution 2817 condemning Iran's attacks on 7 Gulf/Middle Eastern states. Passed 13-0-2 (China, Russia abstaining). First formal multilateral condemnation of Iran's regional attacks. Major diplomatic isolation for Tehran.
impact: Iran diplomatically isolated at UN level. Even Russia and China did not vote against. Provides legal framework for potential collective defense actions.

sources.name: UN Press

title: Netanyahu Calls for Ground Offensive and Regime Change in Iran
description: Netanyahu said at evening press conference that regime change requires a 'ground component.' First explicit Israeli call for ground invasion of Iran. Also claimed Iran can no longer enrich uranium — unverified by IAEA. Addressed Iranian people: 'We stand by your side.'
impact: Ground invasion talk represents potential massive escalation. Regime change as stated war aim could extend conflict indefinitely.

sources.name: CNBC
Haaretz

title: Haifa Oil Refinery Hit by Intercepted Missile Shrapnel
description: Shrapnel from intercepted Iranian ballistic missile struck Israel's largest oil refinery (Bazan Group) in Haifa. First infrastructure damage to Israeli energy sector. Fires extinguished quickly; 45-minute power outage. Raises concern about air defense saturation.
impact: Even intercepted missiles causing infrastructure damage demonstrates vulnerability of critical Israeli energy infrastructure.

sources.name: Al Jazeera

title: Qatar Expels Iranian Military — Diplomatic Rupture After LNG Strike
description: Qatar declared Iranian military and security forces persona non grata after Ras Laffan strike, giving 24 hours to leave. First Gulf state to take such action since war began. Qatar had maintained neutrality but called attack a 'flagrant violation of sovereignty.'
impact: Qatar's diplomatic break signals collapse of Iran's last major Gulf relationship. Removes key backchannel for potential negotiations.

sources.name: Al Jazeera
Euronews

title: Israel Strikes Caspian Coast — First Attack on Bandar Anzali
description: IDF struck Bandar Anzali on Iran's Caspian Sea coast for the first time, destroying 5 missile boats, a headquarters, and shipyard. Represents deepening of strikes into previously untouched Iranian territory. Part of 200+ target strike package.
impact: Strikes reaching Caspian coast means no part of Iran is safe from IDF reach. Eliminates Iran's Caspian naval presence.

sources.name: Alma Research Center

title: Hegseth: 'Largest Strike Package Yet' — Pentagon Requests $200B Supplemental
description: Defense Secretary Hegseth announced the largest strike package to date will hit Iran. Pentagon requesting $200B war supplemental from Congress — far exceeding initial $50B draft. 7,800+ targets already hit. Hegseth set no timeframe for war's end.
impact: $200B request signals long-term war planning. No timeframe statement contradicts Trump's earlier 'ending soon' remarks.

sources.name: CBS News
Al Jazeera

title: Israel Conserving Air Defense Interceptors — Not Shooting Down All Cluster Bomblets
description: IAF making tactical decisions to let some Iranian cluster submunitions through without interception to conserve limited interceptor supply. Half of Iranian missiles carry cluster warheads with ~24 bomblets each. 90% interception rate for populated-area targets.
impact: Reveals strain on Israel's air defense capacity after 20 days of war. Cluster munitions overwhelming standard interception protocols.

sources.name: Times of Israel

title: Dubai Crude Hits Record $166.80 — Oil Market Records Shattered
description: Middle East benchmark Dubai crude hit an all-time record of $166.80/barrel. European jet fuel surged to $220/barrel, diesel breached $200/barrel. Goldman Sachs warned Brent could surpass 2008 all-time high of $147.50. Brent briefly hit $119 intraday before settling at $108.65.
impact: Physical oil and refined product markets hitting records exceeding futures benchmarks. Signals severe supply shortage beyond futures speculation.

sources.name: US News

title: IRGC Spokesperson Gen. Naeini killed — 4th senior official this week
description: IDF killed IRGC spokesman Brig. Gen. Naeini in airstrike on Tehran/Karaj. 4th senior Iranian leader killed this week after Larijani, Soleimani, and Khatib.
impact: IRGC command degraded. 75% of launchers destroyed. Coordination capacity diminished.

sources.name: Al Jazeera

title: Israel strikes Tehran on Nowruz — sacred holiday attacks
description: IDF launched wave of strikes on Tehran during Nowruz (Persian New Year). Signals willingness to escalate without regard for cultural/religious considerations.
impact: Potential to radicalize Iranian population. International condemnation expected.

sources.name: Haaretz

title: Iran expands Gulf energy attacks — Kuwait, Saudi, UAE, Bahrain all hit
description: Iran hit Kuwait Mina Al-Ahmadi (2nd day), Saudi SAMREF Yanbu, fired at Dubai (intercepted), Bahrain (shrapnel). All Gulf energy producers under active fire.
impact: Gulf energy infrastructure under sustained attack. Oil spiked $119.11. All Gulf states in conflict zone.

sources.name: US News

title: Pentagon requests $200B supplemental — war costs spiral
description: Pentagon asked $200B+ supplemental. First 12 days $16.5B (CSIS). GOP divisions. Federal debt $39T. Combined war costs ~$146B.
impact: Fiscal escalation. Congressional opposition. S&P 4th weekly decline.

sources.name: Washington Post

title: DNI Gabbard: Iran NOT rebuilding enrichment — war justification undermined
description: DNI testified Iran not rebuilding enrichment before war. Joe Kent resigned NCTC: 'no imminent threat.' Undercuts administration's casus belli.
impact: Legal/political justification undermined. Could fuel opposition and criticism.

sources.name: Foreign Policy

title: Araghchi rejects ceasefire — no diplomatic off-ramp
description: FM Araghchi rejected ceasefire: 'We do not want this scenario repeated.' Negotiations off the table. War likely to continue indefinitely.
impact: No diplomatic resolution path. Humanitarian crisis deepening. 3.2M displaced.

sources.name: Time

title: Nowruz strikes on Tehran — 4th senior IRGC official killed this week
description: Israel launched strikes on Tehran on Nowruz (March 20) killing IRGC spokesman Naini — the 4th senior official in a week. Combined with Nowruz-eve kills of Ahmadi-Moghaddam, Gharish, and Dehrood, Iran's command structure has lost five senior officials in 48 hours. New Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei issued his first public statement.
impact: Further degradation of Iranian command structure on its most significant cultural holiday. Mojtaba Khamenei forced to issue defiant first statement. Iran continues attacks on Gulf.

sources.name: Al Jazeera
NPR

title: Trump-Netanyahu rift: US demands halt to Iran energy site strikes
description: President Trump publicly demanded Israel stop attacking Iran's energy infrastructure. Trump: 'The United States knew nothing about this particular attack [on South Pars].' Netanyahu confirmed: 'President Trump asked us to hold off on future attacks, and we're holding out.' First major public rift between US and Israel over targeting strategy.
impact: Signals divergence in US-Israel war objectives. Limits future Israeli escalation on energy targets but Trump threatens massive retaliation if Qatar attacked again.

sources.name: CBS News
NPR

title: US to unsanction 140M barrels Iranian crude — major economic pivot
description: Treasury Secretary Bessent announced the US may lift sanctions on ~140 million barrels of Iranian crude stranded on tankers, using Iran's own oil to cap prices. This represents a major reversal of Iran sanction policy in the middle of an active war. Combined with earlier Russian oil sanctions waiver, the US has unlocked ~260M barrels of previously sanctioned oil.
impact: Could provide 10-14 days of price relief. Sets precedent for war-driven sanction flexibility. Signals US prioritizing domestic gas prices over maximum economic pressure on Iran.

sources.name: The Hill
CNBC

title: Trump calls NATO 'COWARDS' — sharpest attack on alliance since war began
description: President Trump posted on Truth Social calling NATO allies 'COWARDS' for not helping reopen the Strait of Hormuz and declared 'Without the U.S.A., NATO IS A PAPER TIGER!' The attack came after six Western allies (UK, France, Germany, Italy, Netherlands, Japan) offered only post-ceasefire Hormuz assistance. Trump's outburst marks the sharpest deterioration in US-NATO relations since the war began and raises questions about the future of US alliance commitments in Europe.
impact: Deepens rift between US and European allies. May accelerate European defense autonomy discussions. Complicates Hormuz reopening coordination and signals Trump will pursue bilateral rather than multilateral approach.

sources.name: Bloomberg
Al Jazeera

title: IDF claims air supremacy over Iran — plans 3+ more weeks to Passover
description: The IDF publicly announced it has achieved air supremacy over most of Iran, having destroyed 100+ air defense systems, 120+ detection systems, and approximately 70% of Iran's ~500 ballistic missile launchers. IDF Brig. Gen. Defrin stated the campaign will continue for at least three more weeks through Passover (April 12). Simultaneously, the US deployed an additional 2,500 Marines and the USS Boxer ARG, bringing US regional troop levels to ~50,000 — signaling operational readiness for potential Kharg Island or Hormuz action.
impact: If accurate, Iran's missile threat reduced by 70% but not eliminated. 3+ weeks additional campaign signals no imminent ceasefire. US Marine deployment suggests Hormuz ground/amphibious option being prepared.

sources.name: Times of Israel
Stars and Stripes

title: Iran fires ballistic missiles at Diego Garcia — unprecedented 4,000km IRBM capability revealed
description: Iran fired two intermediate-range ballistic missiles at Diego Garcia, the joint US-UK base in the British Indian Ocean Territory, in the most far-reaching Iranian strike of the war. The attack required a ~4,000 km range — double Iran's previously claimed 2,000 km IRBM capability. The strike came hours after UK PM Starmer authorized the US to use Diego Garcia for strikes on Iranian missile sites. One missile failed mid-flight; a US Navy destroyer fired an SM-3 interceptor at the second. Neither missile hit the base. The attack opens a new geographic front and demonstrates previously hidden Iranian strategic missile capabilities.
impact: Highest-range Iranian strike in history. Demonstrates unknown IRBM capability. Opens Indian Ocean as new front. Signals Iran may target US global bases beyond Persian Gulf. Prompted US/UK emergency consultations.

sources.name: Jerusalem Post
GB News

title: Trump signals 'winding down' while Pentagon prepares ground force options — contradictory signals
description: President Trump signaled the US may be approaching the end of military operations in a Truth Social post on Day 21, while simultaneously blasting NATO allies as 'COWARDS' for refusing to help open the Strait of Hormuz. However, the Pentagon was simultaneously preparing detailed plans for potential ground operations including 82nd Airborne deployment scenarios, JSOC nuclear material extraction plans, and the departure of USS Boxer with 2,500 Marines. The disconnect between Trump's public 'winding down' rhetoric and active military escalation planning represents a significant strategic ambiguity.
impact: Strategic ambiguity increases uncertainty for allies and adversaries. Pentagon ground force preparations — if leaked to Iran — could trigger preemptive escalation. NATO rupture deepens as Trump publicly attacks allies during active conflict.

sources.name: CBS News
Bloomberg

title: Iran fires cluster missiles at Rishon Lezion daycare and Jerusalem Old City — new pattern targeting civilian zones
description: Iran fired ballistic missiles with cluster warheads at central Israel on Day 22. A cluster munition struck an empty daycare in Rishon Lezion. Debris from an intercepted missile landed near the Temple Mount and Al-Aqsa Mosque in Jerusalem's Old City, damaging a road and wall in the Jewish Quarter. This follows a pattern of cluster munition use against civilian-density areas, and marks the first time Iranian missile fragments have struck near Islam's third-holiest site. The IDF said it intercepted most rounds but is selectively allowing some cluster bomblets to fall to conserve interceptor supply.
impact: First Iranian missile debris to land near Al-Aqsa Mosque — potential trigger for broader Islamic world reaction. Cluster munition use in civilian areas could trigger international legal action. IDF's interceptor conservation strategy signals depletion concerns.

sources.name: Haaretz Day 22 liveblog
Times of Israel liveblog

title: Natanz nuclear enrichment facility struck — Iran's primary centrifuge complex hit
description: US and Israeli forces struck the Natanz uranium enrichment facility on Day 22 — Iran's primary centrifuge complex and the symbolic heart of its nuclear program. Iranian state media agency Tasnim reported 'no radioactive leakage.' The strike escalates the nuclear dimension of the conflict.
